findings.title: Documented project structure does not match repository reality
findings.observed: AGENTS.md & CLAUDE.md document a per-agent service decomposition (agent-spec, agent-codegen, agent-audit, ...). The actual repo collapses that into a single `services/orchestrator` LangGraph + a small set of workers (audit, codegen, compile, deploy, simulation). Sixteen of seventeen documented paths do not exist. New contributors and AI agents are misled.
findings.expected: Project Structure section in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md reflects the real `apps/{api-gateway,studio}` + `services/{orchestrator,...}` layout, with a clear note that per-agent services were rolled into the orchestrator graph.
findings.fix_action: Replace the Project Structure block in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md with the real layout (this PR), and remove unused .cursor/* references that the same document mandates as Pre-Action checks.

findings.title: AGENTS.md mandates Pre-Action reads of `.cursor/rules/`, `.cursor/wiki/`, `.cursor/skills/`, `.cursor/llm/` — none exist

evidence.agents_md_lines: Mandatory Pre-Action (Global) section in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md

findings.observed: Every AI agent is told to read `.cursor/...` and `llms.txt` before any code change. None of those resources exist in the repo, and the only LLM manifest is `llm.txt` (singular). This is a dead instruction surface that produces false-positive 'I followed the protocol' confidence in agents.
findings.expected: Either provide the documented `.cursor/*` directories OR remove the Pre-Action requirement from AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md so it does not silently fail.
findings.fix_action: Replace the Pre-Action block with one that points at real files (AGENTS.md, llm.txt, docs/, .github/agents/*) and drop the `.cursor/*` requirement (this PR).

findings.title: "Spec Lock" is a documentation/marketing concept, not a real schema

code_matches: services/roma-service/main.py:50 ('Turn this HyperAgent spec prompt into a structured Spec Lock JSON')
missing: no class/interface named SpecLock anywhere in services/, packages/, apps/
no Pydantic SpecLock model — only a generic `SpecModel(BaseModel)` in roma-service

findings.observed: `Spec Lock` is referenced in docs as a foundational schema/dictionary, but the actual implementation is a free-form LLM prompt asking the model to produce 'Spec Lock JSON'. There is no validation schema, no versioning, no shared type. Whatever the LLM emits is what flows downstream.
findings.expected: Either define a real `SpecLock` schema (Pydantic + Zod mirror in api-contracts) and validate every roma/spec output against it, OR rename the doc claim to match reality (e.g. 'spec prompt → unstructured spec JSON').
findings.fix_action: Tracked as F-003 in findings; out of scope for a single PR but landing a smaller follow-up: tag the existing `SpecModel` as the canonical Spec Lock contract, mirror it into `packages/api-contracts`, and validate roma output. Remove the `packages/spec-dictionary` reference from AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md (this PR).

findings.title: Pipeline stage strings duplicated between TS package and Python orchestrator with comment-only sync

evidence.sync_rule: packages/workflow-state/src/pipeline-states.ts:2 — `Keep in sync with services/orchestrator nodes and workflow responses.`

findings.observed: TS package enumerates 13 canonical stages; orchestrator stores `current_stage` as a bare `str` with no enum. The only sync mechanism is a docstring. Same class of bug as the previously-fixed `KNOWN_PATH_PREFIXES` in PR #441 (gateway).
findings.expected: Add a parity test (TS or pytest) that statically checks `current_stage` writes in `services/orchestrator/nodes.py` against `PIPELINE_STAGES`. Same pattern as `apps/api-gateway/src/knownPaths.test.ts`.
findings.fix_action: Add `packages/workflow-state/src/__tests__/stage-parity.test.ts` that scans `services/orchestrator/**/*.py` for `current_stage =` literals and asserts each is in `PIPELINE_STAGES`. (This PR.)

findings.title: Auth-bootstrap secrets check leaks no upstream proof beyond verifier-side success

evidence.context: After the SIWE/thirdweb size caps added in #458, the next gap is that the gateway issues a JWT regardless of whether the orchestrator has a corresponding identity record.

findings.observed: The gateway mints `rt=<jwt>` based purely on signature/SIWE verification. It does not require a successful upstream identity-create round-trip before setting the cookie. A correctly-signed but unknown-to-orchestrator wallet still gets a usable Bearer token.
findings.expected: Either: (a) require orchestrator `/api/v1/identity` lookup before issuing the cookie, OR (b) explicitly document that the cookie is signed-by-gateway only and orchestrator route handlers MUST re-validate identity. Today both behaviors coexist without a single-owner contract.
findings.fix_action: Track as F-005; small follow-up PR. No code change in this PR — adding a forced upstream call needs a feature flag and product decision.

findings.title: Gateway proxy `selfHandleResponse: true` re-implements 5xx body shape but still leaks raw bodies on 4xx

evidence.lines: createProxyOptions onProxyRes — only rewrites body for `status >= 500`

findings.observed: 5xx responses are normalized to `{error, message, requestId}`. 4xx responses pass through whatever the orchestrator returned, which means the public API shape is non-uniform. Studio downstream often assumes `error/message` keys but FastAPI emits `detail`.
findings.expected: Normalize 4xx the same way 5xx is normalized (or relay verbatim) so Studio and the SDK can rely on a single error envelope.
findings.fix_action: Tracked; not fixed in this PR to avoid breaking existing 4xx contract assumptions in Studio without a parallel update.

findings.title: Gateway public-paths set treats `/auth/bootstrap` AND `/api/v1/auth/bootstrap` as public — duplicate enumeration
findings.observed: The gateway only mounts `/api/v1/auth/bootstrap`. The bare `/auth/bootstrap` entry is leftover from before the `/api/v1` migration; it is dead unless an undocumented legacy mount exists.
findings.expected: Drop `/auth/bootstrap` from GATEWAY_PUBLIC_PATHS, or add the legacy mount and a parity test.
findings.fix_action: Removed `/auth/bootstrap` (no `/api/v1` prefix) from GATEWAY_PUBLIC_PATHS in this PR after grepping all gateway mounts to confirm no consumer.

findings.title: Orchestrator imports `approve_spec_legacy_router` and mounts it unconditionally

evidence.import: services/orchestrator/api/__init__.py — `approve_spec_legacy_router` is one of the runs_registry exports

findings.observed: A router explicitly named `_legacy` is mounted in production. The naming declares it as legacy with no removal date or deprecation header.
findings.expected: Either rename to drop the legacy suffix (commit it as canonical) or wrap in a feature flag and emit a deprecation header to clients.
findings.fix_action: Add a `Deprecation: true` header middleware around the router and add an integration test that asserts the header. (Out of scope for this audit-only PR; tracked.)

findings.title: `apps/studio/proxy.ts` and `apps/api-gateway/src/proxy.ts` both implement orchestrator forwarding with different rules
findings.observed: Two independent proxy implementations exist. Studio's dev proxy can mask gateway-side bugs in development (e.g., header forwarding, error shape). The dev experience can succeed while production fails.
findings.expected: Studio proxy should either delegate fully to gateway or share a contract test that asserts identical behavior on the same inputs.
findings.fix_action: Add `apps/studio/lib/__tests__/proxy-vs-gateway-contract.test.ts` that fixtures the same input through both proxies and diffs the resulting outbound URL/headers/auth handling.

findings.title: BYOK validation surface is split across gateway, orchestrator, and Studio with three header-forwarding contracts

evidence.studio: apps/studio/lib references `byok` flow in chat hooks

findings.observed: Three places independently parse / validate LLM keys with their own envelope rules. Drift between the gateway BYOK envelope and the orchestrator llm_keys envelope is currently invisible to tests.
findings.expected: Single Zod (TS) + Pydantic (Py) schema for BYOK payloads, generated from `packages/api-contracts/openapi`. Both gateway and orchestrator import from the contract.
findings.fix_action: Tracked. Fix is non-trivial and crosses Python ↔ TS boundary; sequencing into a follow-up after audit.

findings.title: `services/orchestrator/agents/exploit_simulation_agent.py` reads `DEFIHACKLABS_PATH` env without existence check
findings.observed: When `DEFIHACKLABS_PATH` is unset, the agent silently falls back to a relative path that almost certainly doesn't exist in production containers, then degrades to no-finding without telling the caller.
findings.expected: If the corpus is missing, return an explicit `exploit_simulation_unavailable` flag in `AgentState` and surface it to the UI; do not silently produce empty findings that look like 'no exploit risks'.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: Multiple Dockerfiles for the same services (Dockerfile.orchestrator vs Dockerfile.orchestrator.monorepo, etc.)
findings.observed: Production compose uses `.monorepo` variants; `local` compose uses the bare variants. The `.lite` and bare variants are shadowed in production but still maintained, which guarantees drift over time.
findings.expected: Pick one canonical Dockerfile per service and move the variant logic into build args.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: Studio + gateway both hold a list of "public" / unauthenticated paths
findings.observed: Studio's middleware allow-list is independently maintained from the gateway public-paths. A page that's public in the UI but not in the gateway will silently 401, and vice versa.
findings.expected: Single source: Studio middleware imports the gateway public-paths and translates them to UI route equivalents.
findings.fix_action: Tracked. Mitigated by `packages/api-contracts` already exporting GATEWAY_PUBLIC_PATHS — fix is a 1-import change in middleware.

findings.title: `packages/agent-os` ships its own RipgrepAdapter + SearchIntentRouter that duplicate `apps/studio/lib/tools/searchService.ts`
findings.observed: The same TODO/FIXME-pattern grep is implemented twice with the same regex. Studio uses neither imports `agent-os`. Either Studio should consume agent-os, or agent-os/search/ is unused product surface.
findings.expected: Decide canonical owner, drop the other.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: `MAX_AUTOFIX_CYCLES = 3` hardcoded in workflow_state.py — autofix loop bound is duplicated as runtime guard elsewhere
findings.observed: Hardcoded constant. nodes.py and pipeline.py reach for the same bound; if the wrong site is checked, autofix can run unbounded.
findings.expected: Single import-site for the constant; assert in tests.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: `approve_spec_legacy_router` mounted but no test covers the legacy approval path's parity with the canonical path
findings.observed: A legacy router is in production with zero test coverage. Removing or refactoring it is unsafe.
findings.expected: Pytest fixture that hits the legacy path with the same payload as the canonical and asserts equivalent persisted state.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: `packages/api-contracts/src/generated/schema.ts` line 1369-1370 contains an `unused`/`legacy` marker on a generated type
findings.observed: Generated file carries hand-edited or lingering markers, suggesting drift between `openapi/` source and `generated/`.
findings.expected: Regenerate from openapi and assert no diff in CI.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: `x402` mentioned in 103 files, but the gateway-side enforcement reads from a single env flag without verifying upstream settlement

evidence.spread: x402 referenced in 103 files (apps, services, packages, infra)

findings.observed: x402 (paid-API) gating is spread across 103 files. The gateway can mark a request as billable, but the orchestrator settlement webhook is a single POST endpoint that updates state with no signature/HMAC check evident from naming. This is the highest-risk gating surface and warrants a dedicated audit.
findings.expected: Single canonical x402 verifier with end-to-end test: gateway forwards billable header → orchestrator settlement HMAC verified → run state transitions → UI billing display matches DB.
findings.fix_action: Tracked as a top-priority follow-up audit. This PR adds findings + a placeholder test in `apps/api-gateway/src/x402.test.ts` if missing.

findings.title: Orchestrator `current_stage` is `str` with no validator — any agent node can write a typo and break UI mapping
findings.observed: AgentState declares `current_stage: str` (no Literal). Any node that types `'desigh'` instead of `'design'` will pass typing and silently leave the UI in `idle`. This is exactly the failure mode the workflow-state package warns about in its docstring.
findings.expected: Promote `current_stage` to `Literal[...]` mirroring `PIPELINE_STAGES`, OR add a `set_current_stage(state, stage)` setter that validates against the canonical list.
findings.fix_action: Three layers of defence: (a) canonical `PIPELINE_STAGES` frozenset of all 19 stages + `set_current_stage(state, stage)` validator in services/orchestrator/workflow_state.py that raises `ValueError` on unknown stages; (b) all 15 active `state['current_stage'] = ...` writes across services/orchestrator/nodes.py (12 sites including 3 multi-line conditional writes), services/orchestrator/workflow.py (1 site), and services/orchestrator/agents/debate_agent.py (1 site) migrated to `set_current_stage(...)` — the validator now gates writes at runtime; (c) TS-side parity harness in apps/api-gateway/src/pipelineStageParity.test.ts that scans every `current_stage = "..."` / `state["current_stage"] = "..."` / `"current_stage": "..."` write across services/orchestrator/**/*.py and asserts each literal is in PIPELINE_STAGES — fails CI on a typo even before runtime. Initial-dict-literal sites in workflow.py:335 (`"current_stage": "spec"`) and api/runs_registry.py:138 (`"current_stage": "design"`) are left as-is; they are guarded by the parity harness because both literals are in PIPELINE_STAGES.

findings.title: Gateway tests cover 86 cases but none assert end-to-end audit/sim/deploy gating because the orchestrator is not exercised

evidence.tests: 10 test files, 86 tests — all unit-level, all mock the orchestrator
evidence.missing: no integration test that boots both gateway and orchestrator and exercises a single workflow lifecycle

findings.observed: Local tests are healthy (`pnpm test` green) but they cannot catch drift between the gateway proxy rules and the orchestrator routes. The previously-fixed `KNOWN_PATH_PREFIXES` issue (PR #441) is exactly this class of bug.
findings.expected: Add a docker-compose-driven integration suite in CI that exercises at least: bootstrap → BYOK → workflow create → spec → audit (gated) → deploy.
findings.fix_action: This PR adds an `audit/scripts/route-probe.ts` script that, when run against a live stack, asserts every path in `ApiPaths` returns a non-stub response. Used in `audit/08-runtime-proof/` as the harness skeleton; full integration suite tracked.

findings.title: `apps/studio` middleware reads JWT from cookie but does not re-validate signature server-side; relies on gateway
findings.observed: Studio middleware uses cookie presence as a proxy for auth. If the gateway is unreachable but the cookie is still set, the UI will render "signed in" while every API call 502s.
findings.expected: Either render an explicit unreachable-backend banner when gateway pings fail, or only rely on UI auth state from a successful `/api/v1/health/signin` call.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: `services/orchestrator/store.py` carries 3 distinct `legacy` markers — duplicate stores still wired
findings.observed: Three sites in the workflow store carry legacy/deprecated comments. db.py:195,197,1091 has similar markers.
findings.expected: Each legacy marker classified: removable, quarantineable, or canonical. Add tests proving the legacy path is not exercised before removal.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: Three `validate-pr` CI checks run on every PR — historical entries pile up after retitle
findings.evidence: Observed on PR #458: 3 `validate-pr` rows; only the latest is meaningful.
findings.observed: Old `validate-pr` runs persist in the checks list after PR title edits, creating false-fail noise.
findings.expected: Workflow should `cancel-in-progress: true` on PR title/branch update.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: `pipelines.py` (orchestrator) `@router.post('/generate')` accepts a free-form prompt without size/shape validation prior to LLM dispatch
findings.observed: Mirrors the SIWE/thirdweb size cap finding from PR #458, but on the prompt side. A 2 MB prompt forwards to the LLM agent stack and burns BYOK credits before any check.
findings.expected: Add a `MAX_PROMPT_LEN` constant (e.g. 32 KB) and reject oversized prompts with `400 PROMPT_TOO_LARGE` before dispatch.
findings.fix_action: Add Pydantic validator + 413/400 path. (Out of scope for this audit-only PR; tracked.)

findings.title: agent-registry / a2a / erc8004 / user-templates / artifacts proxy mounts forward to orphaned orchestrator routers — Studio actively calls these and was 404'd

evidence.orchestrator: services/orchestrator/api/agent_lifecycle.py defines registry_router, a2a_router, erc8004_router with 25+ endpoints; services/orchestrator/api/user_templates.py defines router + artifacts_router with 7 endpoints; NEITHER file was imported by services/orchestrator/api/__init__.py and NEITHER router was passed to app.include_router(...) in services/orchestrator/main.py
studio_callers: apps/studio/components/agents/RegistryAgentsPanel.tsx — gates ERC-8004 sync UI behind NEXT_PUBLIC_ERC8004_SYNC_ENABLED feature flag (default off) — UI _knew_ the upstream was missing
evidence.unit_test_evidence: services/orchestrator/tests/unit/test_agent_lifecycle_api.py mounts the routers in a private FastAPI() — proves the routers work in isolation but not in production

findings.observed: Gateway forwarded /agent-registry, /a2a, /erc8004, /user-templates, /artifacts traffic to /api/v1/<mount> on the orchestrator; the orchestrator returned 404 because the routers were never imported in api/__init__.py or mounted in main.py. Studio calls these endpoints today.
findings.expected: Mount the routers in main.py and export them from api/__init__.py.
findings.fix_action: Add a parity test in `apps/api-gateway/src/orchestrator-routes.contract.test.ts` that statically scans `services/orchestrator/main.py` `include_router(*)` calls vs gateway mounts, and fails if any gateway mount has no upstream router. (This PR.)

findings.title: `packages/agent-os` is a sizable package with internal tests but no consumer in `apps/*`

evidence.consumers: grep `@hyperagent/agent-os` across apps/services/packages: only internal tests reference it

findings.observed: Whole package shipped, no app imports it. It's not dead in the JS sense (tests pass) but it's product-dead.
findings.expected: Either wire it into a Studio panel/feature or remove it.
findings.fix_action: Tracked.

findings.title: `/user-templates` and `/artifacts` proxy mounts forward to orphan orchestrator routers (sibling of F-025, found during fix)

evidence.orchestrator: services/orchestrator/api/user_templates.py defines router (prefix /api/v1/user-templates) and artifacts_router (prefix /api/v1/artifacts) with seven endpoints; module was never imported by services/orchestrator/api/__init__.py and never mounted in main.py

findings.observed: Same drift class as F-025 — routers exist with full Pydantic validation (TemplateFile, MAX_PACKAGE_BYTES = 2 MB), Pinata pin path, and Supabase indexing, but production never mounted them. Studio calls these endpoints today and was 404'd.
findings.expected: Mount the routers in main.py and export from api/__init__.py.
findings.fix_action: Imports + app.include_router(...) added in services/orchestrator/api/__init__.py and services/orchestrator/main.py. Static parity test in apps/api-gateway/src/orchestratorRouteParity.test.ts now also asserts these prefixes.

blockers.title: Full Docker compose stack run not completed in this pass
blockers.reason: Building 18 Dockerfiles (orchestrator.monorepo, agent-runtime.monorepo, compile.monorepo, audit, simulation, deploy, storage, sandbox-docker, roma, vectordb, hyperagent-tools, etc.) on a fresh runner takes ~30 min and requires real Supabase + Upstash + thirdweb credentials per `.env.example`. Without those, the orchestrator exits at startup. Code-level tests (86 gateway + 16 monorepo packages, all green) and route-static-parity tests provide partial runtime proof.
blockers.evidence: audit/08-runtime-proof/00-api-gateway-tests.txt (86/86 green); .env.example lists 30+ required values; compose file has health-check dependencies.
blockers.harness_added: audit/08-runtime-proof/route-probe.ts (skeleton): when run against a live stack with `BASE_URL` set, hits every entry in `ApiPaths` and reports per-route status/shape mismatches. Keeps the harness in-tree so the next pass can run it without re-discovery.
blockers.next_action: Run `make up-local` with the credentials populated, then `pnpm tsx audit/08-runtime-proof/route-probe.ts` and commit the resulting JSON to `audit/08-runtime-proof/01-route-probe.json`.
